name: Skybrush Server
description: Industry-standard open-source drone show server. Manages fleets, executes choreographies, handles time sync, LED control, geofencing. Python/Trio async. Supports PX4 + ArduPilot via MAVLink.

name: Skybrush Live (GCS Frontend)
description: React/TypeScript GCS frontend for Skybrush. Real-time map with drone positions, LED preview, formation editor, show timeline, safety controls. Connects to Skybrush Server.
ndaa_note: CollMot Robotics, Budapest, Hungary. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: Skybrush Studio for Blender
description: Blender add-on for designing and validating drone show choreographies. Animate point objects in 3D, assign LED colors, export to Skybrush-compatible format. Collision detection, safety validation.
ndaa_note: CollMot Robotics, Budapest, Hungary. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: MDS (MAVSDK Drone Show)
description: All-in-one PX4 drone show + smart swarm. MAVSDK-based multi-drone coordination with Docker SITL, React GCS dashboard, SkyBrush import, LED control, leader-follower clustering. Free for <10 drones.
ndaa_note: Open-source ArduPilot swarm software. Origin unverified — individual contributor. Verify per contract.

name: DSSE (Drone Swarm Search Environment)
description: PettingZoo-based multi-agent RL training environment for SAR drone swarm missions. Drones search for shipwrecked people using dynamic probability matrices. Published in JOSS + arXiv (2307.06240). pip install DSSE.

environments.DroneSwarmSearch: Maritime SAR — drones search for shipwrecked people on dynamic probability matrix. Gaussian dispersion model with drift vector. 9 discrete actions (8 directions + SEARCH). Reward: +1 for finding target, 0 otherwise.
environments.CoverageDroneSwarmSearch: Coverage mission — drones must visit all cells on a probability grid generated by Lagrangian particle simulation (OpenDrift). Uses real ocean/wind data. Reward: +1 per new cell searched, +60 completion bonus, -0.2 per step.

architecture.action_space: Discrete(9): LEFT, RIGHT, UP, DOWN, UP_LEFT, UP_RIGHT, DOWN_LEFT, DOWN_RIGHT, SEARCH

simulation.probability_matrix: Gaussian probability field centered on disaster position. Drifts with configurable vector. Dispersion increases over time (configurable increment). Numba JIT-compiled for speed.
simulation.particle_simulation: OpenDrift-based Lagrangian particle simulation. 50,000 particles, configurable radius. Uses real ocean current and wind data for realistic drift patterns.
simulation.time_step: Derived from drone_speed and cell_size — faster drones get fewer environment steps per real second.

wrappers: AllPositionsWrapper: Returns all drone positions in observation

ppo_cnn.model: Dual-branch: Conv2d(1→16, 8x8) → Conv2d(16→32, 4x4) → FC(256) for probability matrix + FC(512→256) for position → Concat → FC(256) → Policy + Value heads

decentralized_ppo.description: Each drone has its own policy network (1:1 agent→module mapping). Same CNN architecture but independently trained per agent. MultiAgentRLModuleSpec.

rl_algorithms.ppo_cnn_lstm: PPO with CNN + LSTM for temporal context (sequence-aware search)
rl_algorithms.ppo_cnn_comm: PPO with CNN + inter-drone communication channel
rl_algorithms.ppo_mlp: PPO with MLP (no CNN) — position-only baseline
rl_algorithms.greedy_heuristic: Greedy: each drone moves toward highest probability cell. Collision avoidance by waiting. Baseline for RL comparison.
rl_algorithms.a_star_coverage: A* path planning for coverage mission

specs.org_description: Projeto Final de Engenharia (Capstone) — Insper, São Paulo, Brazil. Engineering + CS capstone projects including DSSE (drone swarm SAR), NVIDIA Jetbot autopilot, RISC-V CPU, RFID testing.

NVIDIA_Jetbot_Autopilot.description: Autonomous car MVP on Jetson Nano. Lane detection (OpenCV yellow mask + linear regression), YOLOv3-tiny stop sign detection, MQTT pub/sub architecture, DeepStream SDK, TensorRT. Brazilian traffic sign recognition.
NVIDIA_Jetbot_Autopilot.hardware: Jetson Nano + Jetbot chassis + USB camera

techniques.lane_detection: Yellow mask (LUT thresholding on R+G-B channel) → morphological open → contour centroids → linear regression for lane line → steering gain from centroid offset
techniques.sign_detection: YOLOv3-tiny (416x416, conf>0.2) filtered for 'stop sign' class → MQTT publish 'STOP' command
techniques.road_following: Hough line transform on white mask → angular coefficient filtering (0.62-1.3 for right, -1.3 to -0.62 for left) → vanishing point estimation → MQTT steering commands
techniques.architecture: Perception (camera) → Detection (YOLO/lane) → MQTT broker → Controller (motor commands). Fully decoupled via pub/sub.

ndaa_note: Brazil-origin academic project + Embraer. Not adversary-nation but verify per contract.

name: ORCUS (Autonomous Swarm Kamikaze System)
description: Fully autonomous swarm kamikaze drone system. YOLOv12 detection, ray-ground intersection geo-localization, EKF multi-drone sensor fusion, Hungarian attack assignment, 7-step handshake attack protocol, IBVS terminal guidance. ArduPilot SITL + ROS + Gazebo.

features: Ray-Ground Intersection (RGI) geo-localization from monocular camera
Web-based operator control panel (radar, map, ownership)

vision.camera: ROS topic per drone port, 1280x720, HFOV=1.2rad (~69°), 30fps

swarm.coordinator: 1410 lines — target assignment, Hungarian matching, ownership management
swarm.fusion_engine: 648 lines — two-layer EKF: duplicate merge + cross-drone fusion, dynamic merge threshold (base=8m + sigma*2), Mahalanobis gating (3-sigma), covariance-weighted position fusion, coast mode with velocity prediction
swarm.protocol: 7-step attack handshake (ASSIGN→LOCK→APPROVE→CONFIRM→ECHO→VALIDATE→ATTACK), immutable state protection, backoff spam throttle
swarm.battlespace: Web UI state provider, radar view, target ownership, drone state aggregation, display position smoothing (alpha=0.35)
swarm.target: TrackedTarget with 50+ attributes: covariance matrix, drone-local ID mapping, group membership, ownership state (FREE/OWNED/RESERVED/HANDOFF), coast mode, observation quality scores
swarm.ownership: Sticky assignment with handoff policy (30 consecutive frames, 1.5x quality ratio, 10s cooldown)

mission.attack_fsm: 6 states: IDLE→ECHO→WAIT_CONFIRM→CENTERING→DIVING→IMPACT. Mid-attack guard prevents target swap. Zombie lock failsafe. Phase timeouts: echo=5s, center=10s, global=30s
mission.ibvs_guidance: Image-Based Visual Servoing for GPS-independent terminal attack. 3 phases: STARTUP (Vx=1.5m/s, gentle 5° pitch) → APPROACH (ramp to 8m/s, 10° pitch, ease-in-out curve) → TERMINAL (max speed, 15° pitch). Low-pass filters on yaw/vz/vx. Ghost mode (0.5s coast on target loss). Lateral body-frame correction. Pitch compensation for fixed camera mount.
mission.detection_processor: RGI (Ray-Ground Intersection) geo-localization from monocular camera + drone attitude. Covariance-aware: cross-range and radial uncertainty scales with depression angle. Edge compensation for FOV distortion.
mission.navigation: PID waypoint tracking (Kp=0.9, Ki=0.02, Kd=0.12). GPS median filter (window=7). Stuck detection (0.12m threshold, 8s timeout). Fine approach deceleration (7m threshold, 0.35 scale).

name: CoFlyers
description: General platform for collective flying of swarm drones. MATLAB/Simulink simulation + real hardware (Crazyflie, Tello). Bio-inspired swarming behaviors, formation control, collective decision-making.
ndaa_note: MICROS Lab, ENAC (French civil aviation school), Toulouse, France. EU/NATO. NDAA compliant.

name: Crazyswarm2
description: Gold standard for swarm research on Crazyflie nano-quads. Scales to 49+ drones. ROS2 (Humble). Trajectory tracking, formation control, real-time visualization. Motion capture integration.
ndaa_note: IMRC Lab, University of Southern California, Los Angeles CA. US academic. NDAA compliant.

name: Insper NVIDIA Jetbot Autopilot
description: Autonomous ground vehicle MVP on Jetson Nano. Lane following via OpenCV (yellow lane detection with LUT thresholding + linear regression steering), stop sign detection via YOLOv3-tiny, MQTT pub/sub decoupled architecture, DeepStream SDK + TensorRT inference. Brazilian traffic sign dataset. Relevant to drone CV pipelines — same Jetson + YOLO + steering pattern applies to visual servoing.

features: Lane detection (LUT yellow mask + contour + linear regression)
Hough line road following with vanishing point

lane_detection.method: Color channel arithmetic (R*0.5 + G*0.5 - B) → LUT threshold (32-48) → morphological open (11x11) → contour centroids → linear regression
lane_detection.steering: Centroid offset from frame center → proportional gain (0.2) + angular coefficient correction

road_following.method: Hough line transform on white HSV mask → angular coefficient filtering (right: 0.62-1.3, left: -1.3 to -0.62) → vanishing point from line intersection → moving average smoothing (window=20)

ndaa_note: Insper + NVIDIA academic partnership. Brazil origin. Verify per contract.

name: OpenIPC FPV
description: Alternative open-source firmware for IP cameras repurposed as FPV air units. Runs on cheap IP camera SoCs (HiSilicon, Goke, SigmaStar, Ingenic, etc.) with wifibroadcast (WFB-NG) for low-latency video. Turns a $15 IP camera board into a digital FPV transmitter. Lower latency than OpenHD on RPi (80ms 1080p60 vs 100-150ms) because the IP camera SoC has a dedicated hardware encoder. MIT license.

fpv_socs_recommended.SSC338Q: Dual-core, future-proof, best performance for new firmware updates
fpv_socs_recommended.SSC30KQ: Single-core cut-down of SSC338Q, budget option
fpv_socs_recommended.gk7205v200: Budget FPV, well-tested, most wiki documentation

cameras_sensors: Sony IMX307 (most popular, good night vision with F0.95 lens)

latency.note: Lower than OpenHD RPi (100-150ms) because IP camera SoC has dedicated HW encoder

SBC_GS.description: SBC-based ground station using Buildroot, maintained by OpenIPC FPV dev team

PixelPilot.description: Android app for Snapdragon devices, decodes RTP video stream

wfb_ng.description: WifiBroadcast Next Generation — the core video transport layer

majestic.description: Proprietary streamer (replaces GStreamer on IP camera SoCs)

vs_openhd.latency: 60-100ms vs 100-150ms (RPi) — OpenIPC wins on HW encoder
vs_openhd.flexibility: Limited to supported IP camera SoCs vs RPi ecosystem
vs_openhd.ease_of_use: Harder initial setup (SPI flash, UART, SoC-specific) vs easier (SD card flash)
vs_openhd.cameras: IP camera sensors (IMX307, etc.) vs CSI cameras (Arducam, RPi HQ)
vs_openhd.community: Growing rapidly, strong Telegram presence vs established, broader ecosystem
vs_openhd.military_note: OpenIPC explicitly prohibits military use in their license

unique_features: Runs on $8-15 IP camera boards (cheapest FPV air unit possible)
1.7W total power draw (lowest of any digital FPV)
Hardware H.265 encoding on camera SoC (no separate encoder needed)
F0.95 fast lens support for night FPV on IMX307
ipctool for identifying unknown IP camera hardware
SPI NOR flash direct programming (burn utility)
APFPV mode — simple WiFi AP mode for phone viewing

high-power-fpv.note: 120fps H.264 for racing — lower quality, minimum latency

common_params.mcs_index: 1 — MCS rate index (lower = more robust, less throughput)
common_params.channel_161: 5805 MHz — clean channel, standard for FPV

smolrtsp.description: Lightweight RTSP 1.0 server for embedded. Zero-copy parsing. H.264 + H.265 RTP. TCP + UDP. Used by Majestic streamer.
smolrtsp.use_in_forge: Can replace GStreamer RTSP for edge AI deployments on Jetson/APB. Smaller footprint than full GStreamer pipeline for video-only streaming.

ipctool.description: Identifies IP camera hardware — SoC, sensor, flash, network chip. Single static binary (musl). Run on any camera to discover what's inside.
ipctool.use_in_forge: Useful for identifying unknown camera modules on drone payloads. Drop ipctool binary onto any Linux camera board to get SoC/sensor/flash info.

divinus.description: Open-source multi-platform IP camera streamer. HAL architecture supports 20+ SoC families. RTSP + RTMP + UDP + fMP4 + MJPEG + JPEG snapshot. Night mode (auto IR cut/LED). OSD overlay. Audio (MP3 encode). ONVIF discovery.

modules.server.c: 1499 lines — HTTP API server with endpoints: /api/jpeg (snapshot), /api/mjpeg (stream), /api/mp4 (recording), /api/audio, /api/night (IR mode), /api/osd (overlay), /api/status, /api/cmd, /api/record, /api/time. Plus ONVIF discovery.
modules.media.c: 950 lines — encoder pipeline: video capture → HAL encode → RTSP + RTMP + UDP + MP4 + HTTP. Audio: PCM → MP3 (libshine) → RTSP + RTMP + MP4.
modules.stream.c: 456 lines — UDP/RTP streaming with multicast support, client management, RTP header construction
modules.rtsp/: 3592 lines — full RTSP 1.0 server: session management, RTP packetization (H.264/H.265), thread pool
modules.night.c: 155 lines — auto day/night switching via GPIO: IR cut filter + IR LED + grayscale mode
modules.jpeg.c: 143 lines — JPEG snapshot with per-platform HAL abstraction
modules.region.c: 555 lines — OSD overlay regions (text, bitmap, PNG)
modules.onvif.c: 397 lines — ONVIF device discovery and media profile responses

http_api_endpoints./api/night: Night mode control (IR cut, IR LED, grayscale)
http_api_endpoints./api/status: System status (uptime, encoder state, stream stats)

use_in_forge: HAL pattern: portable SoC abstraction for multi-platform camera code — same API, different hardware. Relevant for AI/CV guide when supporting multiple companion computers.
HTTP API: /api/jpeg endpoint is a clean pattern for grabbing camera frames for AI inference without GStreamer. Hit the endpoint, get a JPEG, run YOLO.
Night mode: GPIO-based IR cut/LED switching pattern is directly applicable to thermal camera integration on drones.
RTSP server: lighter than GStreamer for embedded video streaming. The rtsp/ directory is a self-contained 3592-line RTSP implementation.
UDP/RTP streaming with multicast: the stream.c pattern (456 lines) is a clean reference for implementing WFB-NG style video broadcast.

ndaa_note: OpenIPC, Italy-based community. EU/NATO allied-nation. NDAA compliant.
